description: Multidimensional taxonomy covering Deepfake Detection methodologies, Generation regimes, Impact and Intent.

entry.description: Content fully generated by AI without a prior real base. Source: Unilim/arXiv
Modification of existing media via face-swap, lip-sync, or re-enactment. Source: Unilim
Use of recent generative technologies leaving different signatures than GANs. Source: arXiv:2411.19537
Combined synthesis of audio, image, video, and text. Source: arXiv:2411.19537
Examination of pixel-level anomalies, sensor noise, or compression artifacts. Source: ResearchGate
Detection of motion inconsistencies or flickering between successive video frames. Source: ResearchGate
Absence of eye blinking or heart rate anomalies detected via photoplethysmography. Source: ResearchGate
Visible boundary traces or inconsistent lighting reflections. Source: ResearchGate
Analysis of audio/lip-sync desynchronization. Source: ResearchGate
Analysis of specific image features such as texture, color inconsistencies, and geometric face shape. Source: Neha Sandotra
Detection using deep learning models to identify frame-level inconsistencies. Source: ResearchGate/Neha Sandotra
Deliberate intent to manipulate opinion or harm a target. Source: Unilim.
Humorous usage where the fake nature is often assumed or pedagogical. Source: Unilim
Use of synthetic imagery to destroy an individual's credibility. Source: Unilim/SciSpace
Historical reconstruction or pedagogical usage. Source: Unilim
Production of fake academic evidence or hijacking of scientific expertise. Source: Oxford Academic
Misuse of AI for profiling, emotion classification or ethno-phrenology. Source: SciSpace
Intentional undermining of trust between citizens and institutions. Source: Oxford Academic
Targeting public figures or private citizens for impersonation. Source: Unilim
